name: Miaoli County (Taiwan)

colors.symbolism: the hills, tea plantations and forests of the county
the warmth and vitality of the Hakka-majority population

description: A white field charged at the centre with a stylised emblem in green and red, depicting the character 苗 (miao, 'sapling/seedling') of the county's name with floral and agricultural motifs.
history: Miaoli County (Miaoli Xian) is a county on Taiwan's northwest coast and is one of the principal homelands of Taiwanese Hakka culture. The current county flag has been in use since the late 20th century. The emblem stylises the character 苗 ('sapling') from 苗栗 (Miaoli), reflecting the county's agricultural and tea-growing heritage.

features.symbolism: stylised character 苗 ('sapling/seedling', from 苗栗/Miaoli) with floral and agricultural motifs, evoking the county's hills, tea plantations, and Hakka heritage
